gene_description: ALK (ALK Receptor Tyrosine Kinase) encodes a receptor tyrosine kinase. Chromosomal translocations involving ALK (most commonly EML4-ALK) create fusion oncogenes present in 2-7% of NSCLC. ALK fusions are oncogenic drivers and confer excellent sensitivity to ALK tyrosine kinase inhibitors.

source.title: Anaplastic lymphoma kinase inhibition in non-small-cell lung cancer
source.finding: Crizotinib achieved 57% response rate in ALK-positive NSCLC; established ALK as actionable target in lung cancer

TT.phenotype: Tag allele associated with ALK fusion-positive tumors
TT.effect: Genetic marker linked to ALK fusion prevalence; predicts ALK fusion status and excellent ALK inhibitor response
TT.recommendation: If ALK fusion confirmed by FISH/NGS, initiate ALK inhibitor therapy. Crizotinib first-line or next-generation ALK inhibitors (alectinib/ceritinib) for improved CNS penetration.

TC.effect: Intermediate frequency; may be associated with ALK fusion status
TC.recommendation: Perform ALK fusion testing (FISH, NGS, or immunohistochemistry). If positive, use ALK inhibitor. If negative, evaluate for other fusions (ROS1, NTRK).

CT.effect: Intermediate frequency; may be associated with ALK fusion status
CT.recommendation: Perform ALK fusion testing (FISH, NGS, or immunohistochemistry). If positive, use ALK inhibitor. If negative, evaluate for other fusions (ROS1, NTRK).

CC.effect: Lower frequency of ALK fusion association; standard treatment paradigm applies
CC.recommendation: ALK fusion testing still indicated if clinical suspicion. Evaluate for EGFR mutations, KRAS, and other drivers. Consider immunotherapy if no driver mutation.

clinical_significance: ALK fusion testing mandatory in NSCLC. Present in 2-7% of lung adenocarcinomas. Crizotinib revolutionized treatment; subsequent next-generation ALK inhibitors improved CNS penetration and overcome resistance.
